data.narrative:
uAvionix is a Series B-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Paul Beard that has raised $55M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 5 public-market aerospace peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies, with three same-stage, same-sector comparables directly applicable to valuation.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $55M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $104.5M. Peer-median methodology uses three same-sector Series B companies—Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M)—producing a median valuation of $400M. The peer range spans $274M to $1.3B, reflecting material variance in this early-stage cohort. Public-comp anchoring (sector P/S median of 59.8x across 5 tickers) cannot be applied due to lack of disclosed revenue for the target.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $89M to $460M, with a median of $252M. The wide spread reflects the early stage and heterogeneity within the peer set. Key uncertainties include uAvionix's revenue trajectory relative to peers, customer concentration, and technology differentiation within the crowded aerospace sector. Stage-progression and peer median methods diverge materially, suggesting the need for additional diligence on growth assumptions and customer traction before final valuation commitment.
